column3: Texas Bluebonnet Seeds - Native Texas Wildflower - 0.25 lb, Covers 500 sq ft | Nature's Seed
column4:
Bring the iconic Texas spring to your landscape with Texas Bluebonnet Seeds (Lupinus texensis) from Nature's Seed. The official Texas state flower, bluebonnets transform fields and roadsides into breathtaking blue-violet displays each spring.

Why you'll love Texas Bluebonnets
Authentic Texas heritage wildflower with deep blue-violet blooms tipped in white
Easy fall planting produces stunning spring color from March through May
Native legume fixes nitrogen and improves soil health naturally
Drought-tolerant once established, thriving in lean, well-drained Texas soils
Self-seeding perennial returns year after year with minimal effort
Attracts pollinators including native bees, butterflies, and hummingbirds

Perfect for meadows, roadside plantings, open fields, and native landscapes across USDA Zones 4-9. Plant in fall for best results. Scarify seeds before sowing for faster germination.

column3: Texas Native Wildflower Mix - 8-Species Native Blend - 0.5 lb, Covers 1,000 sq ft | Nature's Seed
column4:
Create a vibrant Texas wildflower meadow with the Texas Native Wildflower Mix from Nature's Seed. This expertly blended 8-species mix features Texas Bluebonnet, Indian Paintbrush, and six additional native species selected for continuous color from March through October.

Why you'll love this mix
Authentic 8-species Texas native blend with Bluebonnet at 22% of the mix
Season-long color from spring through fall with staggered bloom times
Adapted to Texas soils and climate for reliable establishment
Drought-tolerant native species require minimal irrigation once established
Supports Texas pollinators including monarch butterflies and native bees
Simple scatter-sow planting makes it easy to establish even on large areas

Ideal for meadows, roadsides, ranch entries, and restoration projects across Texas. Plant in fall for best spring color. Thrives in full sun with well-drained soil.

column3: Texas Pollinator Wildflower Mix - Bee & Butterfly Habitat Blend - 0.5 lb, Covers 1,000 sq ft | Nature's Seed
column4:
Support Texas pollinators with the Texas Pollinator Wildflower Mix from Nature's Seed. This specially formulated blend combines native Texas wildflowers selected for maximum nectar and pollen production, providing critical food sources for bees, butterflies, and hummingbirds throughout the growing season.

Why you'll love this mix
Native Texas species selected specifically for pollinator support
Extended bloom period provides nectar from early spring through fall
Supports honeybees, native bees, monarch butterflies, and hummingbirds
Drought-tolerant once established for low-maintenance pollinator habitat
Adapted to Texas heat, alkaline soils, and low-rainfall conditions
Scatter-sow planting method makes establishment simple

Perfect for pollinator gardens, meadows, conservation areas, and property borders. Contributes to monarch butterfly corridor and native bee habitat restoration across Texas.

column3: Texas Native Lawn Mix - Low-Water Native Turf Blend - 5 lb, Covers 1,000 sq ft | Nature's Seed
column4:
Build a drought-proof Texas lawn with the Texas Native Lawn Mix from Nature's Seed. This 3-species native grass blend is dominated by Buffalograss at 70%, with Blue Grama at 20% and Curly Mesquite at 10%, specifically formulated for Central and West Texas alkaline soils.

Why you'll love this lawn mix
Uses 30-40% less water than Bermuda and other conventional turf grasses
Native 3-species blend built for Texas alkaline soils and extreme heat
Buffalograss-dominant for dense, low-growing turf with excellent wear tolerance
No fertilizer needed once established for truly low-maintenance lawn care
Deep root system survives Texas drought and heat stress naturally
Mow just 2-3 times per season or leave as a natural shortgrass lawn

Ideal for home lawns, commercial landscapes, and municipal turf in USDA Zones 5-9. Plant in late spring when soil temperatures reach 60F or above for best establishment.

column3: Texas Native Prairie Mix - Rangeland & Conservation Blend - 10 lb, Covers 40,000 sq ft | Nature's Seed
column4:
Restore native Texas prairie with the Texas Native Prairie Mix from Nature's Seed. This 8-species blend combines native grasses and forbs specifically selected for Texas rangeland improvement, prairie restoration, and conservation programs including CRP and EQIP.

Why you'll love this prairie mix
8 native Texas grass and forb species for diverse, resilient prairie establishment
Formulated for Texas rangeland improvement and prairie restoration projects
Deep-rooted native perennials provide excellent erosion control on slopes and disturbed sites
Drought-tolerant species thrive in Texas heat with minimal supplemental irrigation
Qualifies for USDA conservation programs including CRP and EQIP
Supports native wildlife habitat and biodiversity restoration

Ideal for rangeland restoration, conservation programs, highway rights-of-way, and large-scale native habitat projects. Plant at 10 PLS lb per acre. Best results with fall or late-winter dormant seeding.

column3: Mexican Primrose (Pinkladies) - Native Texas Pink Wildflower - 0.25 lb, Covers 750 sq ft | Nature's Seed
column4:
Add iconic Texas pink roadside color to your landscape with Mexican Primrose (Oenothera speciosa) from Nature's Seed. Also known as Pinkladies, this native perennial creates spreading carpets of delicate pink blooms from April through October across Texas roadsides and meadows.

Why you'll love Mexican Primrose
Native Texas perennial that creates sweeping carpets of soft pink blooms
Extended bloom period from April through October for months of color
Extremely drought-tolerant once established, requiring no supplemental water
Thrives in poor, sandy, and rocky Texas soils where other flowers struggle
Spreads via rhizomes and self-seeding for reliable coverage expansion
Attracts butterflies, bees, and other beneficial pollinators

Perfect for meadows, xeriscapes, roadsides, and open natural areas. Surface sow seeds in fall for best spring establishment. Note: vigorous spreader best suited to open areas, not manicured garden beds.

column3: Drummond Phlox Seeds - Texas-Native Annual Wildflower - 0.25 lb, Covers 1,000 sq ft | Nature's Seed
column4:
Add multicolor spring beauty to your Texas landscape with Drummond Phlox Seeds (Phlox drummondii) from Nature's Seed. The only annual phlox species native to Texas, Drummond Phlox produces vibrant clusters of red, pink, purple, and white blooms from March through May.

Why you'll love Drummond Phlox
The only annual phlox native to Texas with multicolor blooms in red, pink, purple, and white
Perfect bluebonnet companion plant with identical growing requirements
Easy fall planting produces reliable spring color from March through May
Thrives in full sun to partial shade with well-drained, slightly acidic soil
Self-seeding annual returns each spring with minimal effort
Compact 6-12 inch height ideal for borders, beds, and meadow plantings

Ideal for wildflower meadows, borders, rock gardens, and companion planting with Texas Bluebonnets. Plant seeds in fall (September through December) at 0.25 lb per 1,000 sq ft. Cover lightly as seeds require darkness for germination.

The only annual phlox native to Texas with multicolor blooms in red, pink, purple, and white
Perfect bluebonnet companion plant with identical growing requirements
Easy fall planting produces reliable spring color from March through May
Thrives in full sun to partial shade with well-drained, slightly acidic soil
Self-seeding annual returns each spring with minimal effort
Compact 6-12 inch height ideal for borders, beds, and meadow plantings

Ideal for wildflower meadows, borders, rock gardens, and companion planting with Texas Bluebonnets. Plant seeds in fall (September through December) at 0.25 lb per 1,000 sq ft. Cover lightly as seeds require darkness for germination.
